description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a brushed metal front panel and wooden side panels, giving it a classic and robust appearance. The front features a horizontal blue-lit frequency display, a large tuning knob on the right, and several smaller control knobs and switches below the display. The top surface has two sets of ventilation slits, likely for heat dissipation. The rear panel is equipped with multiple RCA input/output ports and speaker terminals labeled for left and right channels, indicating its use as an audio receiver or amplifier. The sides are plain wood, adding to its retro aesthetic, while the overall build suggests a sturdy, high-quality construction. This type of device would typically be found in home audio setups, garages, or community centers within urban environments.
description: A vintage radio receiver with a brushed metal front, blue-lit frequency display, multiple control knobs, and wooden side panels. The rear features numerous audio ports and speaker terminals.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue-lit frequency display, large tuning knob, several smaller control knobs, and switches on a brushed metal panel.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain wooden panel and the protruding control knobs at the front edge.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing multiple RCA input/output ports, speaker terminals for left and right channels, and a black metal back panel.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the plain wooden panel and the protruding tuning knob at the front edge.
